Class Monster

java.lang.Object
component.Unit
component.Monster
public class Monster extends Unit
Represents an enemy monster unit.

A Monster is a Unit with predefined base stats depending on its type, and can be scaled according to the current stage to control difficulty.

Supported types (normalized to 1..3):

  • 1 - Slime (higher HP/DEF scaling)
  • 2 - Crossaint (higher ATK scaling)
  • 3 - Cabbage (balanced scaling)

After construction, base stats are applied automatically via applyBaseStats(). Stage scaling can be applied using scaleToStage(int).

  • Field Details

    • type

      private final int type
      Monster type (normalized to 1..3).

  • Constructor Details

    • Monster

      public Monster(int type)
      Constructs a Monster of the given type. The type will be normalized to range 1..3.
      Parameters:
      type - monster type identifier

  • Method Details

    • normalizeType

      private static int normalizeType(int t)
      Normalizes a type value into range 1..3.
      Parameters:
      t - raw type value
      Returns:
      normalized type in {1,2,3}

    • getNameByType

      private static String getNameByType(int type)
      Returns the monster display name for the given type.
      Parameters:
      type - raw type value (will be normalized)
      Returns:
      monster name by type

    • applyBaseStats

      private void applyBaseStats()
      Applies base stats according to this monster's type. This method sets attack, max HP, current HP, and defense.

    • scaleToStage

      public void scaleToStage(int stage)
      Scales this monster's stats to match the specified stage.

      Stage is clamped to at least 1. Scaling multipliers are type-dependent.

      After scaling, the monster is healed to full HP (spawn behavior).

      Parameters:
      stage - current stage number (>= 1)

    • attack

      public double attack(Unit target)
      Performs a basic monster attack using its current attack value.
      Parameters:
      target - target unit to attack
      Returns:
      actual damage dealt
